data.title: Citi sells Russian unit to Renaissance Capital
data.description:
Citigroup (C) on Wednesday announced the close of the sale of its Russian subsidiary, AO Citibank, to the investment bank Renaissance Capital.

In December last year, the banking giant had said that its board approved a plan to sell the unit, which conducts its remaining Russian operations.

The previously-disclosed $1.1B after-tax loss related to the sale had impacted the bank's Q4 2025 EPS.

The divestiture is expected to benefit Citi's common equity Tier 1 capital in Q1 2026 by ~$4B. The projected benefit is driven by the deconsolidation of associated risk-weighted assets, a reduction in disallowed deferred tax assets, and the release of associated currency translation adjustment loss.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Wednesday: HSY, ADI, RIOT, FVRR
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher before the bell as Wall Street looked toward a positive open with technology stocks regaining momentum.

Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Riot Platforms (RIOT) gained 5% in early action after hedge fund Starboard Value delivered a letter urging strategic action at the bitcoin mining and infrastructure company. The activist investor is pushing Riot to complete deals while demand for its sites remains strong and to further improve its corporate governance and operations.

Analog Devices (ADI) rose 6.6% in premarket trade after fiscal first-quarter results and outlook beat estimates. For the second quarter of fiscal 2026, the semiconductor company expects revenue of $3.5 billion, plus or minus $100 million, compared to a consensus of $3.23 billion. Analog Devices also guided adjusted EPS to $2.88, plus or minus $0.15, significantly above the $2.46 analyst consensus.

Fiverr International (FVRR) dropped 19% in early Wednesday trading after the Tel Aviv-based freelance marketplace issued mixed fourth-quarter results and posted 2026 guidance below expectations. Revenue rose 3.4% year over year to $107.2 million, but marketplace revenue declined 2.7% to $71.5 million during the quarter. Annual active buyers fell 13.6% to 3.1 million at the end of the period.

Hershey’s (HSY) slipped 0.4% before the opening bell amid controversy surrounding its iconic Reese’s Peanut Butter Cup brand. Brad Reese, grandson of Reese’s inventor H.B. Reese, publicly accused the company of quietly degrading the candy’s core recipe and misleading consumers about what they’re buying.

data.title: Microsoft set to receive 20% of total OpenAI revenue until 2032: report
data.description:
• Microsoft (MSFT) is set to receive 20% of OpenAI's (OPENAI) total revenue until 2032, The Information reported.
  • Some of the payments will come in the later years of the agreement, the news outlet added, citing the company's reworked deal this past fall. The 20% of total revenue to Microsoft is in exchange for OpenAI being allowed to work with other compute providers and not providing Microsoft with the first right of refusal. The original partnership saw Microsoft entitled to 20% of OpenAI's total revenue through 2030.
  • OpenAI and Microsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Quant ratings on Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square Q4 holdings: BN, UBER, AMZN, GOOG, META
data.description:
Pershing Square Capital Management, the fund run by billionaire hedge fund manager Bill Ackman, revealed its Q4 holdings for 2025 on Tuesday, valued at approximately $15.53B, the latest 13F filing showed.

Comparing Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square holdings from Q4 2024 to Q4 2025 reveals a bold shift into the 'Magnificent Seven' and the complete exit from several long-term holdings.

Notable additions included significant stakes in Uber Technologies (UBER), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta Platforms (META), which together added over 42.49M shares to the portfolio. Hertz Global (HTZ) also bought as a tactical position, with over 15.2M shares. In Q4 2025, Pershing Square increased its stake in Brookfield (BN) by 76% as compared to Q4 2024, making it the top asset in the portfolio.

The firm completely exited Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG), Nike (NKE), and Canadian Pacific Kansas City (CP) and reduced positions in Alphabet (GOOGL) Class A by 83% and Hilton Worldwide (HLT) by 44% from Q4 2024 to Q4 2025.

The manager posted a 0.88% dip over the past year, significantly trailing the S&P 500's 11.7% return during the same period, according to data from stockcircle.com.

As of December 31, 2025, the New York-based hedge fund maintained a highly concentrated portfolio of just 11 positions. Here is a closer look at those holdings alongside their current Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings.

  • Brookfield Corporation (BN) – Quant rating: 3.40 – Hold
  • Uber Technologies (UBER) – Quant rating: 3.05 – Hold
  • Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) – Quant rating: 3.39 – Hold
  • Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) – Quant rating: 3.48 – Hold
  • Meta Platforms (META) – Quant rating: 3.38 – Hold
  • Restaurant Brands International (QSR) – Quant rating: 3.91 – Buy
  • Howard Hughes Holdings (HHH) – Quant rating: 4.93 – Strong Buy
  • Hilton Worldwide Holdings (HLT) – Quant rating: 2.82 – Hold
  • Seaport Entertainment Group (SEG) – Quant rating: 2.83 – Hold
  • Hertz Global Holdings (HTZ) – Quant rating: 3.16 – Hold

data.title: How high can the Nasdaq-100 climb in 2026? Here’s what prediction markets say
data.description:
After powering to fresh highs earlier this year, the Nasdaq-100 (NDX) has experienced bouts of volatility as investors rotate between high-growth technology names and more defensive areas of the market.

Prediction market Kalshi provides insight into current sentiment, outlining a range of potential upside targets for 2026. Based on the latest probabilities, traders see a strong likelihood of additional gains, though expectations vary as price objectives climb higher. 

According to Kalshi data, an 87% probability is assigned to 25,600 or above, while 26,000 or higher also carries an 87% chance. 

However, probabilities begin to taper as projections stretch toward more aggressive milestones. A rally to 28,000 or above carries a 54% chance, while 29,000 or higher is priced at 56%. The most ambitious forecast — 30,000 or above — currently holds a 36% probability. 

The distribution suggests traders anticipate further gains but remain measured about extreme upside. See the full percentage breakdown below based on Kalshi data: 

  • 87% — 25,600 or above
  • 87% — 26,000 or above
  • 85% — 27,000 or above
  • 84% — 25,800 or above 
  • 84% — 26,200 or above
  • 81% — 26,400 or above 
  • 79% — 26,600 or above
  • 79% — 28,200 or above 
  • 69% — 27,400 or above 
  • 68% — 26,800 or above
  • 66% — 27,800 or above 
  • 63% — 27,200 or above 
  • 62% — 27,600 or above
  • 57% — 28,400 or above 
  • 56% — 29,000 or above 
  • 54% — 28,000 or above
  • 54% — 28,600 or above
  • 50% — 29,500 or above 
  • 48% — 28,800 or above
  • 36% — 30,000 or above 

Nasdaq Focused ETFs: (QQQ), (QQQM), (SQQQ), (TQQQ), (QLD), and (QID).

data.title: CareTrust REIT files for up to $1B at-the-market program
data.description:
CareTrust REIT (CTRE) has filed for an up to $1B at-the-market program with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares under the equity distribution agreement are set to be sold through sales agents, or forward sellers, by means of ordinary brokers' transactions at market prices, in negotiated transactions, or in transactions that are deemed to be an 'at-the-market' offering.

Net proceeds are intended to be used for general corporate purposes, which may include, among other things, future acquisitions, debt repayment, and working capital.

data.title: Five years later, Cathie Wood's ARKK is still down over 50% since its 2021 peak
data.description:
Five years after peaking at $159.70 on February 16, 2021, the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) remains well below its record high, underscoring the volatility that has defined the fund’s recent history.

As of Wednesday, February 18, 2026, ARKK is down 55.9% from that peak, reflecting a prolonged drawdown following its pandemic-era surge. 

The flagship strategy of Cathie Wood has faced mounting scrutiny despite a notable rebound in 2025, when the ETF advanced 35.5% on the year. That recovery, however, has not fully repaired the longer-term damage, and shares are already lower by 8.6% in 2026 as investors rotate away from high-growth technology stocks and into more defensive sectors. 

Wood has consistently defended ARK’s approach, emphasizing a five-year investment horizon built around disruptive innovation platforms such as artificial intelligence, robotics, and genomics. While that long-term thesis remains central to ARK’s strategy, trailing results over the past half-decade have lagged broader technology benchmarks. 

By comparison, the Invesco QQQ Trust, Series 1 ETF (QQQ) has climbed 80% over five years, while the State Street Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) has gained 103.7% during the same period, highlighting the divergence within growth-oriented investing. 

Additionally, see how Wood’s other actively managed ETFs have performed over the past 5-years: 

  • ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX) +54.2% 
  • ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ) +25.6%
  • ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) -33.8%
  • ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (ARKF) -39.9%
  • ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) -73.2% 

ARKX performance is just under 5 years, as it launched on March 30, 2021.

data.title: AutoCanada appoints new CEO, to also serve as interim CFO
data.description:
• AutoCanada (ACQ:CA) appointed Samuel Cochrane as chief executive officer effective immediately.
  • Cochrane will also serve as interim chief financial officer.
  • He joined AutoCanada in August 2024 as CFO and was named Interim CEO in October 2025.

data.title: MKS stock falls, analysts say investors looking for larger beat
data.description:
Shares of MKS (MKSI) fell about 7% premarket on Wednesday after quarterly results and outlook, while analysts pointed out that investors were "looking for a more material beat."

Morgan Stanley kept its Overweight rating on the stock with a $258 price target.

"While the MarQ guide is modestly below our expectations, MKS has exceeded its guidance by an average of 3.4% over the past eight quarters. At this stage, we remain confident the company will report ahead of MSe in 90 days. The GM% outlook is somewhat light, and we may have underestimated the seasonal headwind in the chemistry business around CNY," said analysts led by Shane Brett.

The analysts expect the June quarter to show meaningful sequential acceleration as CNY-related chemistry weakness reverses.

Wells Fargo maintained its Equal Weight rating and $210 price target on the stock.

"While 1Q26 guide in line / modestly above, shares are under pressure AMC as we think investors were looking for a more material beat given peer results & semi cap outlooks; MKS a signif o/p'er YTD at +65% (vs SOX +15%)," said analysts led by Joe Quatrochi.

Chip equipment makers: ASML (ASML) climbed about 2%, while Lam Research (LRCX), KLA (KLAC), and Applied Materials (AMAT) rose around 1% each.

data.title: Genesis Energy launches $500M senior notes offering due 2034
data.description:
• Genesis Energy (GEL) on Wednesday said it has commenced a registered public offering of $500 million in aggregate principal amount of senior notes due 2034.
  • The notes will be co-issued with Genesis Energy Finance Corporation and initially guaranteed by all subsidiaries other than unrestricted subsidiaries.
  • The company said it intends to use the net proceeds to purchase or redeem up to $490 million of its outstanding 7.75% senior notes due 2028, with any remaining proceeds to be used for general partnership purposes, including repaying borrowings under its senior secured credit facility.
  • GEL -1.92% premarket to $17.32.
  • Source: Press Release
